# When It Fails: Generalization in Pictures, and Inductive Bias {#sec-06-generalization-inductive-bias}

::: {.callout-warning}
**Chapter stub.** This chapter is planned but not yet written. The abstract below is its
contract; drafting sources are listed for provenance.
:::

*The signature chapter. We watch an MLP that aces MNIST fall apart when digits shift two pixels — in pictures. Capacity curves, sample complexity, the curse of dimensionality; and the cure: build what you know about the data into the architecture. That idea powers every remaining chapter.*

<!-- draft-sources
seeds:
- `sources/4.1-CNN.tex (curse-of-dim + inductive-bias sections)`
- `sources/ML_concepts_roadmap.tex`
transcripts:
- `m03 lectures`
- `m04_lecture-4-convolutional-neural-networks-cnns_s6xDEvdz3tY.txt (opening)`
inspiration:
- 3Blue1Brown, "Gradient descent, how neural networks learn":
  https://www.3blue1brown.com/lessons/gradient-descent
  (author-cited; echo its visual-first pacing: watch the failure happen before naming it)
note: outline session with the author BEFORE drafting (docs/backlog.md §3)
-->
